name: @joehe71/browser-tools
description: Browser automation tools for Finch — navigate, click, fill forms, take screenshots, read page content, upload files, save PDFs, drag and drop, and more. Powered by agent-browser (Vercel Labs).

finch.name: Browser Tools
finch.description: Control a real browser from Finch: navigate, click, fill, screenshot, read page content, and more — powered by agent-browser (Vercel Labs).
finch.systemPrompt:
When the user needs to interact with a web page, scrape content, take screenshots, fill forms, upload files, save PDFs, drag and drop, or automate browser tasks, use the Agent Browser tools. Each tool wraps the agent-browser CLI.

Setup: If agent-browser is not installed, call `browser_setup` to install it and download Chrome for Testing automatically. This is a one-time setup. If `browser_check` shows agent-browser is not available, always suggest calling `browser_setup` first.

Workflow: Start with `browser_check` to verify the environment. Then `browser_navigate` to open a URL, `browser_snapshot` to get the accessibility tree with element refs, and `browser_click` / `browser_fill` / `browser_select` / `browser_set_checkbox` to interact. Use `browser_screenshot` to visually verify state and `browser_read` to extract readable content. Clean up with `browser_close` when done.

promptGuides.title: Set up Agent Browser
promptGuides.description: Check whether agent-browser is installed and install it if needed.
promptGuides.prompt: Check if agent-browser is installed using /browser_check. If not installed, call /browser_setup to install the agent-browser CLI and download Chrome for browser automation.

promptGuides.title: Scrape a webpage
promptGuides.description: Navigate to a URL and extract its readable content.
promptGuides.prompt: /browser_navigate open https://example.com and /browser_read get the page content
